researcher_notes: Anglo (US/UK/AU/CA + IN-anglo) electronics/maker/DIY sources + major aggregators. Tested ~55 URLs across ~40 distinct domains. Many sources are news-heavy (covering vendor announcements vs step-by-step tutorials) — flagged in `notes`. Only sources verified with HTTP 200 + valid XML + >=5 entries are listed. Where multiple paths existed (e.g., Pimoroni shop atom vs blog WP) the working one is recorded.

sources.name: Circuit Digest
sample_titles_recent: SpikerBot Turns Neural Signals into Real-Time Robotic Motion
A Homemade Approach to Long-Range Night Vision
Unitree Unveils $650,000 Pilotable Mecha Robot
What Happens When You Build a Private Network Just to Track Cows?
Dhruva Space Gets ₹105 Crore Government Grant for Mass-Producible Satellites
sources.notes: Major IN-anglo electronics hub. The RSS shows news+projects mix, but the site itself has a heavy tutorial library (Arduino/ESP32/Raspberry Pi step-by-step). Worth ingesting and filtering by URL path /microcontroller-projects/ or /electronic-circuits/. Robotics overlap is significant — coordinate with robotics category settings.

sources.name: Engineers Garage

sources.notes: Recent items skew toward product announcements / vendor PR. Their tutorial library exists but RSS surfaces news first. Recommend keyword-filter (require 'how to', 'tutorial', 'project', 'guide' in title) or post-fetch URL-path filter.

sources.name: How To Mechatronics

sources.notes: Dejan Nedelkovski's project-focused blog — every post is a deep DIY build with full Arduino code + 3D files. Pure tutorial gold. Low frequency (~1-2/month) but very high signal. PRIORITY TOP-5.

sources.name: Electronics For You

Future AI Systems Could Be Faster
Geometry Powers 3D Surgical Tracking
Water Free Hydrogen Fuel Cell Membrane Survives Extreme Heat

sources.notes: RSS leans news. Site has separate /diy-electronics-projects/ section with real tutorials but RSS doesn't surface them well. Recommend filtering or fetching from a section-specific feed if discoverable. India-anglo English.

Diode Clamping Circuit
Logic Gates Using Transistors
Instrumentation Amplifier
sources.notes: Wayne Storr's reference site. Topic-driven theory tutorials (transistors, amplifiers, gates). Evergreen pedagogical content — perfect for foundational SEO. PRIORITY TOP-5. Low frequency but every post is genuine teaching content.

sources.name: Electronics-Lab

sources.notes: Heavily news-oriented (product launches). Useful for surfacing new ESP32/Pi boards / KiCad libraries that THEN become tutorial fodder. Treat as ingredient signal, not directly publishable tutorial. Borderline qualifies; include for breadth but tag low rank weight.

sources.name: Electronic Clinic

How Dangerous Are Hypersonic Missiles: The World's Fastest Weapons

sources.name: Predictable Designs
sample_titles_recent: STM32 vs GD32 vs CH32: Should You Trust the Cheaper Alternatives?
NEVER Hire a Marketing Agency for Your New Product
Every Development Platform Ranked Worst to Best (for Your Product)

sources.notes: John Teel — product development for hardware entrepreneurs. Strong comparative pieces (STM32 vs GD32 vs CH32 is genuinely useful Spanish translation candidate). Some pure business content needs filtering (marketing, sell-before-build). Recommend require 'STM32|Arduino|ESP32|MCU|board|sensor|microcontroller' in title.

Look for posts that link to learn guides or contain 'how to', 'build', 'project'. Tag low weight to avoid flooding.
